channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: The U.S. Is Pulling Back on Energy Efficiency
item.description: The Trump administration is undoing fuel-saving efforts even as war in Iran, data centers and heat waves are pushing up gasoline and electricity costs.
item.dc:creator: Brad Plumer
item.media:description: A dishwasher at a General Electric plant in Louisville, Ky. The Energy Department has moved to repeal 17 standards for dishwashers, washing machines, kitchen appliances and more.

item.title: Trump Rejected Nearly 6,000 Clemency Applications Amid Hope of Pardon Wave
item.description: Expectations were raised for a raft of pardons in connection with the country’s 250th birthday but the president granted fewer than 20 on July 3.
item.dc:creator: Kenneth P. Vogel and Alan Feuer

item.title: Trump to Impose 50% Tariff on Many Canadian Goods
item.description: The administration will use an untested legal provision to put significant duties on Canadian exports, reigniting a clash with one of America’s biggest trading partners.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson and Ian Austen
item.media:description: The tariffs, which go into effect in 30 days, would be imposed on a range of Canadian exports, including wine and dairy products.

item.title: A New Threat to Global Shipping, and a Huge Drop-Off in Food Stamps
item.description: Plus, when A.I. writes your biography.
item.dc:creator: Tracy Mumford, Will Jarvis, Margaret Kadifa, Jake Lucas, Ian Stewart, Jason DeParle and Kashmir Hill
item.media:description: The Iran-backed Houthi militia in Yemen on Monday said it would impose a maritime blockade on Saudi Arabia, a U.S. ally, in a move that threatened to open a new front in the regional war and further disrupt global energy supplies.

item.title: New UK Prime Minister Burnham Promises Hope and Change. The Hurdles Are High.
item.description: Britain’s new prime minister, Andy Burnham, promised policies that would give average Britons financial “breathing space.” His opponents are already doling out criticism.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
item.media:description: Prime Minister Andy Burnham speaking in front of 10 Downing Street on Monday. “We will make this moment a circuit breaker for Britain,” he said.

item.title: Burnham, UK’s New Prime Minister, Starts With Electricity Tax Cut
item.description: Prime Minister Andy Burnham moved quickly to confront the high cost of living. And President Trump wished him well after a phone call between the two leaders.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: Prime Minister Andy Burnham of Britain outside No. 10 Downing Street on Monday. In a statement, Mr. Burnham said that he wanted “to give people breathing space.”

item.title: Inside Andy Burnham’s 1st Day as U.K. Prime Minister
item.description: As Andy Burnham became Britain’s seventh leader in a decade on Monday, he tried to show that he will govern differently.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle, Michael D. Shear and Megan Specia
item.media:description: King Charles III welcoming Mr. Burnham at Buckingham Palace.

item.title: Houthis in Yemen Edge Closer to Entering U.S.-Iran War
item.description: The Iran-backed Houthi militia in Yemen has declared a Red Sea blockade on Saudi Arabia, potentially opening a new front in the war rippling across the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Ismaeel Naar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Yemeni supporters of the Iran-backed Houthi movement gather during a rally against what the group says are restrictions imposed by the Saudi-led coalition on Houthi-controlled areas of Yemen, in Sana last week.

item.title: Lebanese Troops Deploy in Israeli-Held Village Under New Cease-Fire Plan
item.description: The Israeli military withdrew from the village under a U.S.-brokered deal to transfer control of occupied areas back to Lebanon after months of war. But Hezbollah has rejected the truce.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Lebanese army troops in Zawtar al-Gharbiya, southern Lebanon, after Israeli forces withdrew from the area on Tuesday.

item.title: When Protesters Speak, Zelensky Listens, in the Ukrainian Tradition
item.description: With anger high over the firing of the defense minister, the president’s office is signaling an openness to compromise.
item.dc:creator: Andrew E. Kramer
item.media:description: A photograph released by the Ukrainian Presidential Press Service on Monday showing President Volodymyr Zelensky meeting with Gen. Mykhailo Drapaty, the commander of the Joint Forces, in Kyiv.

item.title: I Thought ‘Menswear’ Was for Other Men. Then I Touched the Perfect Hat.
item.description: Lots of men are geeking out over very expensive clothes — and I became one of them.
item.dc:creator: Sam Anderson

item.title: Once Anti-Abortion, Now a Progressive: The Evolution of a Maine Democrat
item.description: Troy Jackson, who is expected to become his party’s nominee in the state’s marquee Senate race, has long been an economic populist, but his positions on social issues have changed markedly over time.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein and Beth Reinhard
item.media:description: Troy Jackson is expected to become Democrats’ new nominee for the U.S. Senate after his quick consolidation of support from party delegates last weekend prompted his leading rivals to withdraw.

item.title: What We Know About Tuesday’s Arizona Primaries
item.description: Arizona primary voters will chose nominees for governor, secretary of state and for key races that could determine control of the House.
item.dc:creator: Reis Thebault, Kellen Browning and Sejal Govindarao
item.media:credit: Jon Cherry for The New York Times
item.media:description: A polling site in Phoenix in 2024.

item.title: How Trump’s Immigration Crackdown Is Hurting Elder Care
item.description: Retirement communities already grappling with labor shortages are being forced to terminate caregivers at a time when more Americans are living longer.
item.dc:creator: Miriam Jordan
item.media:credit: Ian C. Bates for The New York Times
item.media:description: More than half of 400 nursing homes surveyed in 2023 said they had turned away prospective residents because they lacked enough staff.

item.title: The Ordeal of a 42-Day Hantavirus Quarantine: ‘I Felt Like a Prisoner’
item.description: Eighteen Americans were isolated for weeks in a Nebraska quarantine facility after being exposed to the deadly virus on a cruise ship. Many found the experience traumatic.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:credit: Jordan Gale for The New York Times
item.media:description: Dr. Steve Kornfeld, who was quarantined because of hantavirus exposure, at his home in Bend, Ore., last month.

item.title: How a 911 Call Led to the Nation’s Strictest Trans Bathroom Law
item.description: People in Sandpoint, Idaho, don’t know how to characterize a woman’s brush with a transgender woman in a Y.M.C.A. locker room, but the emergency call that followed is still reverberating.
item.dc:creator: Anna Griffin and Loren Elliott For The New York Times

item.title: How Two Brothers Dug for Dead Relatives: With a Shovel and a Kitchen Knife
item.description: After Venezuela’s devastating earthquakes, Winder and Deivi Delfín dug every day for 26 days to find the bodies of their mother, brother and grandmother.
item.dc:creator: Julie Turkewitz and Adriana Loureiro Fernandez
item.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Winder Delfín, 17, right, and his brother Deivi, 15, during the 24th day of searching for their mother, younger brother and grandmother, who lived on the first floor of a twelve story building that collapsed during the twin earthquakes last month.

item.title: Tuesday Forecast: Millions In Northeast Face Heavy Rain and Powerful Winds
item.description: Forecasters zeroed in on an area from Kentucky to New York as being the most at risk for dangerous weather.
item.dc:creator: Erin McCann, Judson Jones and Nazaneen Ghaffar

item.title: Bill Maher Feuded With Trump. Did He Get the Last Laugh?
item.description: How the late-night host won comedy’s biggest prize — despite the White House’s objections.
item.dc:creator: Benoit Denizet-Lewis
item.category: Kennedy, John F, Center for the Performing Arts
Real Time With Bill Maher (TV Program)
item.media:credit: Emily Shur for The New York Times

item.title: The Grim Lessons of Israel’s War in Gaza
item.description: Israel has not been held accountable for its actions in Gaza. The nation and the individuals and countries enabling it to escape punishment may only benefit.
item.dc:creator: Omer Bartov
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Trump’s Kiss of Death
item.description: On top of everything else, the president is inept.
item.dc:creator: Thomas B. Edsall
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Photo Illustration by Philotheus Nisch for The New York Times

item.title: Lives Lost
item.description: We look at the American casualties in the war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Sam Sifton
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: U.S. Army, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images and Associated Press
item.media:description: Pvt. Isabella Gonzales and First Lt. Tyler James Feehan.

item.title: Seth Meyers Calls Trump ‘The Sports Reaper’
item.description: The late-night host joked that the president’s sports curse had struck again after he backed Lionel Messi of Argentina, which then lost the World Cup final to Spain.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: Cyclospora Spreads Through Human Feces. How Did It Get Into Our Food Supply?
item.description: The large number of cases suggests that excrement entered the food system on a “surprising scale.”
item.dc:creator: Alice Callahan and Caroline Hopkins Legaspi
item.media:description: These cyclospora oocysts were found in a stool sample. The parasite infects the small intestine, causing frequent watery diarrhea that carries its oocysts, or eggs.
